pi_items.source: Verified via task.json description and verifier.py logic explicitly checking for DASHBOARD_NAME.
Verified via task.json prompt instructions which mandate these exact titles.
Verified via the task description which assigns the user the role of Performance Release Engineer.
Verified contextually via the use of EC2 instances, AWS CloudWatch data, and canary release methodologies.
Verified via export_result.sh and verifier.py which outputs and reads the task result at this specific file path.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real server metrics from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset, specifically the realAWSCloudwatch dataset. The expected dashboard name is confirmed to be Canary Release Audit, and the expected graph titles (Legacy vs Canary Baseline, Instantaneous Delta, Net Cumulative Penalty) exactly match the task prompt requirements. The data represents actual EC2 CPU utilization, confirming the data is not synthetic and fits the Cloud Infrastructure industry and Performance Release Engineer occupation.
